title: hypericum punctatum
intro: Hypericum punctatum, also known as spotted St. John's Wort, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers with dark red spots and a woody, four-angled stem. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Hypericum punctatum include reducing anxiety and depression, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the dried herb in tinctures and infusions.
